title: Bridge Essay: Repetitive Religious Chanting Modulates the Late-Stage Brain Response to Fear- a

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Language/Mantra** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Language as mantra — mantra meditation, inner speech, focused attention, verbal repetition.

**Repetitive Religious Chanting Modulates the Late-Stage Brain Response to Fear- and Stress-Provoking Pictures.** — Front Psychol. PMID: 28119651. DOI: 10.3389/fpsyg.2016.02055. Chanting and praying are among the most popular religious activities, which are said to be able to alleviate people'snegative emotions. However, the neural mechanisms underlying this mental exercise and its temporal course have hardly been investigated. Here, we used event-related potentials (ERPs) to explore the effects of chanting the name of a Buddha (Amitābha) on the brain'sresponse to viewing negative pictures that were fear- and stress-provoking.
